name: Sharp
what_it_does: High-performance Node.js image processing library backed by libvips that resizes, converts, and transforms images 4–5x faster than ImageMagick with a chainable async API.
use_cases: Resize and convert uploaded images to WebP/AVIF for optimized web delivery in content pipelines
Generate multiple responsive image variants (thumbnail, medium, large) in a single pipeline pass
Strip EXIF metadata and normalize orientation before storing user-uploaded photos
Composite watermarks or overlays onto product images in e-commerce workflows
Extract image metadata (dimensions, colorspace, ICC profile, DPI) for validation and routing decisions
not_for: Browser-side image processing (Sharp is Node.js only; use Canvas API or WebAssembly alternatives)
Advanced computer vision tasks like object detection or feature matching (use OpenCV instead)
PDF or multi-page document rendering (use a dedicated PDF library)
best_when: You need fast, memory-efficient server-side image transformation at scale with minimal dependencies beyond a prebuilt native binary.
avoid_when: You are running in a serverless environment with strict binary size limits or cannot use native Node addons.

pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 licensed. libvips dependency is also open source (LGPL 2.1+).

performance.rate_limits: None (library — bound by local CPU and memory)

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Throws descriptive Error objects with messages like 'Input file is missing' or 'unsupported image format'; errors are catchable and parseable.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Pipeline operations are pure transforms; same input always produces same output.
known_agent_gotchas: Sharp uses a lazy pipeline — no processing occurs until .toFile(), .toBuffer(), or .pipe() is called; agents must await the terminal method, not the constructor chain.
Prebuilt binaries are platform-specific; cross-architecture Docker builds (e.g., building on ARM for x86) require --platform flags or will fail with a cryptic native addon error.
The .rotate() method without arguments auto-rotates based on EXIF orientation but also strips all EXIF; call .keepExif() first if metadata must be preserved.
Reading from a stream and writing to a stream simultaneously is supported but requires careful backpressure handling; buffer intermediate results when in doubt.
Sharp does not support animated WebP or GIF animation by default — enabling animation support requires passing { animated: true } and may increase memory usage significantly.

security_score_components.security_notes: Processes files locally; validate input paths to prevent directory traversal. libvips has a strong track record of patching image-parsing CVEs promptly.
